frameworks.name: scene structure / spatial regularity
frameworks.one_line: visual brain exploits typical spatial layouts to analyze natural scene content efficiently

frameworks.name: EEG multivariate decoding
frameworks.one_line: category information read out from time-resolved EEG via classification

core_question: Does adherence to typical scene structure facilitate the emergence of scene category information in early visual brain signals?
key_assumptions: natural scenes have predictable spatial structure across categories
EEG signals carry decodable scene category information
the visual system can exploit spatial regularities during early processing
manipulations: scenes presented in typical spatial structure vs disrupted structure
key_findings: scene category was more accurately decoded from EEG when scenes adhered to typical spatial structure
the advantage arose during early visual processing
interpretations: the visual brain leverages real-world spatial structure to efficiently analyze scene content
